finding: FILL MODEL: contrarian entry after >=12bps prior move (microstructure family)

avail_le_55c.note: 34 vs 35 of 49; one-interval slip-definition difference, within tolerance

attacks.lookahead: FAILED TO BREAK: re-fetched 12 sampled markets from CLOB prices-history; the point used as p20 is stamped t0+3..19s in all 12 (BEFORE the ~t0+20s decision), stored values equal refetch 12/12. The '20s' snapshot is really a ~5-19s price, consistent with live entrySec p50=9s. No future info.
attacks.boundary_overlap: PASSED: my independent buffered open-to-open construction (consecutive-candle required) reproduces n=49 and all quantiles exactly; construction follows the brief.
attacks.threshold_sensitivity: ROBUST on the deliverable: thr 9.6bps -> n=81, c20 med .515, avail .80; thr 14.4bps -> n=40, med .525, avail .725. Cap -20% (44c) mechanically collapses availability to ~2% since the distribution sits near 50c, but 55c is the bot's pre-registered revEntryMax, not a fitted parameter.
attacks.multiple_comparisons: LOW EXPOSURE: this is a descriptive cost-input (quantile estimation at pre-registered bot parameters: 55c cap, live entry timing), not a selected hypothesis test; the finding itself disclaims the reversal EDGE (last-third win rate .38-.39, CI .12-.62).

residual_caveats: Join n~30 is small; the 'fast entry ~2c cheaper than minute-mid' point estimate has wide spread (p5 -11.5c, p95 +3.5c); treat as ~1.5-3c, sign robust.
avail 69-71% is computed on minute-bucket mid-ish prices, not asks; live fillability 85% brackets it from above, so 69% skip-modeling is conservative.
Any backtest consuming these quantiles must also model the ~31% of signals priced .555-.60 as SKIPS, exactly as the finding says.
